content_of_post_to_answer: How do people manage to stay focused and perform well under extreme pressure, like during military drills or high-stakes industrial work?
answer: People often assume performance under extreme pressure comes from exceptional courage, but in military drills and industrial work it is usually the result of reducing the need for improvisation. Training builds automatic responses so the mind can spend its limited attention on unusual problems instead of basic actions. I've written more about this pattern on my profile for anyone who wants a deeper look. Pressure does not create skill; it reveals what has already been made reliable.
